mission.title: minio: The cluster is restarted successfully only when all nodes are started.
mission.description: The cluster is restarted successfully only when all nodes are started.. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current minio deployment
steps.description:
Verify your minio version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
helm list -n minio 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working minio installation.

steps.title: Review minio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant minio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl get configmap -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=minio
```
Docker swarm node on one node,  each service has its own volume

steps.title: Apply the fix for The cluster is restarted successfully only when all nodes…
steps.description:
Avoid waiting perpetually for DNS resolution
```yaml
docker stack deploy --compose-file=docker-compose.yaml   minio_test
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl get events -n minio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "The cluster is restarted successfully only when all nodes…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/minio/minio/pull/7355

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with minio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
